pods.title: Wikipedia summary
plaintext: Mount Elbrus is the highest mountain in Russia and Europe. It is a dormant stratovolcano rising 5, 642 m (18, 510 ft) above sea level, and is the highest volcano in Eurasia, as well as the tenth-most prominent peak in the world. It is situated in the southern Russian republic of Kabardino-Balkaria in the western extension of Ciscaucasia, and is the highest peak of the Caucasus Mountains.
